repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 concerns cumulant-based LiNGAM recovery, not residual-score ANM factorization.

published_prior_art.cite: montagna2023nogam, Lemma 1: zero population squared error in predicting a score entry from its conditional-mean residual holds iff the node is a leaf; in the bivariate ANM this is the same zero-set certificate.

repo_prior_art.cite: No ExactID catalogue entry gives this residual-score/KL notation.

published_prior_art.cite: gross1975logarithmic supplies the log-Sobolev entropy--relative-Fisher inequality; conditioning on W and using the translated residual reference gives the displayed bound directly.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry duplicates the Gaussian score-Jacobian leaf calculation.

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score proves the Gaussian-ANM score-Jacobian leaf criterion, including zero diagonal-score-Jacobian variance at a leaf.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry studies finite-sample generic-noise residual-score ordering.

published_prior_art.cite: kpotufe2014 consistency of nonparametric ANM direction inference is a missing finite-sample comparator; zhu2023sample supplies Gaussian SCORE bounds, though neither proves this exact minimax frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: NoGAM's Lemma 1 already gives the generic-noise residual-to-score zero-loss iff-leaf certificate; specifying the optimal residual predictor as the residual density score is not a new identification result.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 gap / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The claim that NoGAM does not state an exact zero-set relation is false: its Lemma 1 states zero population residual-to-score prediction loss iff the coordinate is a leaf.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The KL inequality is the standard conditional application of Gross's log-Sobolev entropy--Fisher-information inequality to the translated residual reference law.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The Gaussian SCORE leaf reduction is covered by rolland2022score's score-Jacobian leaf result.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite-sample survey omits Kpotufe et al. (2014), which gives nonparametric ANM causal-direction consistency and is a closer generic-noise finite-sample comparator than the two listed papers.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 / comparator promise table
novelty_flags.one_line: Peters is labeled a strict tightening, but the theorem only re-expresses reverse exclusion under an extra LSI and does not state a strict frontier or tightening of Peters's identification result.

soundness_flags.label: def:score-residual-defect and all statements using m_{V|W}
soundness_flags.one_line: Conditional means are used in both directions without an integrability condition; ass:forward-anm permits non-integrable f(N_X), so add E|f(N_X)|<infinity (or integrability of both coordinates).

soundness_flags.label: ass:reverse-exclusion
soundness_flags.one_line: This is not Peters's named restricted-ANM identifiability condition: Peters uses a differential Condition 19 plus causal minimality, whereas direct reverse-model exclusion is a conclusion or separately imposed condition.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Forward ANM plus Gaussian child noise permits a linear-Gaussian reversible model, where both J values and both SCORE variances can be zero; neither rule uniquely selects the child without reverse exclusion or an equivalent non-reversibility condition.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / ass:residual-lsi
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The KL calibration assumes, rather than derives, a log-Sobolev inequality for each orientation-specific residual law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: D_{V|W}(P) <= J_{V|W}(P)/(2 kappa) only conditional on the stated residual-LSI regime.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pivot the population kernel away from the NoGAM zero-loss lemma; a viable replacement needs a genuinely new generic-noise finite-sample theorem for a specified estimator and smooth ANM class, with a rate or lower bound benchmarked against Kpotufe et al. and Zhu et al.

Replace the direct conditional-LSI corollary with a non-immediate stability result, such as a sharp converse or an observable lower/upper separation from the reverse-ANM class under a materially wider condition than LSI.

If retaining the Gaussian reduction, add reverse exclusion or explicit non-reversibility and integrability assumptions, and correctly name the reverse-exclusion assumption rather than attributing it to Peters's restricted-ANM condition.

recommended_next_step: Pivot: the three proved population claims are NoGAM/SCORE or a textbook LSI corollary, so a field-tier revision requires a new finite-sample or nontrivial stability kernel rather than polishing this formulation.
